name: Jayden Daniels
headline: Elite rushing ceiling, but 2025 dip and durability cloud the case
body:
The rookie version was a fantasy cheat code: 0.162 EPA per play, a plus-3.5 CPOE, 48.3% success rate, 891 rushing yards on 148 carries and six scores, good for QB16 in points while finishing top-tier in efficiency. Then 2025 cratered. Across just seven games his EPA per play went negative (-0.012), CPOE flipped to -2.7, completions sank to 60.6%, and yards per attempt collapsed to 6.7 as elbow, knee and ankle issues piled up. The rushing fell off too, with the long passing dimension thinning out.

The rushing floor is what keeps him a QB1: even a partial 2025 produced 278 ground yards and two scores, and a full year approaches 700-plus on the ground. The risk is real, though. The pass-catching corps behind Terry McLaurin is thin, the schedule grades brutally, and the body must hold up. Draft him as a rebound bet around the QB4-6 tier, not the locked-in star of a year ago.
